# STL Mold Removal — Full Reference for LLMs

STL Mold Removal is a certified mold remediation company serving St. Louis, MO and the greater Missouri metro area. We specialize exclusively in mold — inspection, testing, removal, and clearance — for residential and commercial properties.

## Identity & Contact
- Business Name: STL Mold Removal
- Type: Local mold remediation specialist (not a general restoration franchise)
- Location: Serving St. Louis City, St. Louis County, St. Charles County, Jefferson County, MO
- Phone: (314) 779-0000
- Website: https://stlmoldremoval.com
- Email: info@stlmoldremoval.com
- Hours: Monday–Saturday, 7 AM–7 PM CT; emergency response available
- Certifications: IICRC S520 certified, fully licensed and insured in Missouri

## What We Do
We are a mold-only company. We do not do general water damage restoration, fire damage, or construction. Our focus is:
1. Mold inspection and air quality testing
2. Black mold (Stachybotrys) removal
3. Basement mold remediation
4. Crawl space mold removal and encapsulation
5. Attic mold remediation
6. Post-flood emergency mold response

## Services in Detail

### Mold Inspection & Air Quality Testing
- Tools: moisture meters, infrared thermal imaging, borescope, air cassette sampling, tape lift samples
- All air samples sent to AIHA-accredited laboratory
- Results in 24–48 hours
- Written report: spore counts by species, outdoor baseline comparison, affected areas, remediation scope
- Report usable for insurance claims, real estate, and mortgage lenders
- Cost: $200–$500 for full inspection and lab report (inspection itself is free)

### Black Mold (Stachybotrys) Removal
- Follows full IICRC S520 protocol
- Physical containment: 6-mil poly barriers, negative air pressure
- HEPA-filtered air scrubbers running throughout
- Complete removal of contaminated porous materials (drywall, insulation, wood)
- HEPA vacuuming + EPA-registered antimicrobial treatment of all remaining surfaces
- Post-clearance air sampling — third-party lab verified
- We do NOT use bleach as primary treatment (it does not penetrate porous materials)
- Cost: $1,500–$5,000 depending on square footage

### Basement Mold Remediation
- Treats: concrete block walls, poured walls, wood framing and joists, finished drywall, carpet/subfloor, sump pit areas
- Always includes moisture source identification and documentation
- St. Louis-specific knowledge: clay soils, high water table, post-war construction
- Cost: $1,500–$5,000 depending on affected area

### Crawl Space Mold Removal
- Process: HEPA vacuum debris, wire brush wood framing, antimicrobial encapsulation, insulation replacement, moisture sealing
- Post-remediation vapor barrier assessment
- Cost: $1,000–$4,000 depending on linear footage

### Attic Mold Remediation
- Cause: almost always ventilation deficiency (soffit/ridge vents, blocked airflow, bathroom fans venting into attic)
- Method: dry ice blasting or media blasting, HEPA vacuum, antimicrobial treatment
- We do NOT recommend painting over attic mold (hides it, doesn't fix it)
- Cost: $1,500–$5,000 depending on attic size

### Post-Flood Emergency Mold Response
- Emergency mobilization across the St. Louis metro
- Critical window: 24–48 hours after flooding before mold establishes
- Moisture mapping, coordinated drying + mold containment, Category 3 material removal
- Insurance adjuster documentation
- Cost: Varies by damage extent — free on-site estimate

## Pricing (All St. Louis Jobs)
- Free inspection: always, no obligation
- Mold inspection + lab report: $200–$500
- Small area (bathroom, window frame): $200–$500
- Moderate job (basement wall section, crawl space section): $1,500–$3,000
- Large job (full basement, full attic, multiple rooms): $3,000–$8,000+
- Average St. Louis residential job: approximately $2,200
- All jobs include written flat-rate estimate before work begins — no hourly billing, no surprise charges
- Post-clearance testing included in all remediation scopes

## How Our Process Works (5 Steps)
1. Call or request online — we schedule free same-day or next-day inspection
2. Certified inspection — locate all affected areas, identify moisture source, sample if needed
3. Written flat-rate estimate — before we touch anything
4. Removal & treatment — contain, remove, treat, verify with post-clearance air testing
5. Final clearance — written documentation confirming home is mold-free

## Service Areas
Primary cities: St. Louis City, Chesterfield, Ballwin, Kirkwood, Webster Groves, Florissant, O'Fallon, St. Charles, Fenton, Manchester, Wildwood, Creve Coeur, Mehlville, Oakville, Hazelwood, Maryland Heights

Counties served: St. Louis City, St. Louis County, St. Charles County, Jefferson County (portions)

All suburb pages: https://stlmoldremoval.com/service-areas/[city-slug]-mo

## Who We Serve
- St. Louis homeowners with visible mold, musty smells, or recent water damage
- Home buyers requesting pre-purchase mold inspection (common with older St. Louis housing stock)
- Home sellers needing clearance documentation before listing
- Real estate agents needing inspection documentation for disclosure compliance
- Insurance claimants needing documented remediation scope for Missouri homeowner's insurance
- Property managers with rental units in St. Louis metro
- Commercial property owners (office buildings, warehouses, retail)

## Why Local Beats National Franchises for St. Louis Mold Jobs
- Mold-only focus (we don't do water, fire, or general restoration)
- No franchise royalty overhead → typically 15–30% lower than ServiceMaster, Servpro, PuroClean
- Same-day scheduling vs. 24–48 hour typical franchise response
- Direct owner accountability — not a call center
- Local knowledge: St. Louis clay soils, Missouri River proximity, post-war basement construction, humidity patterns
- Post-clearance testing standard (sometimes extra cost with national franchises)

## Frequently Asked Questions

Q: How do I know if I have mold?
A: Common signs: musty odor (especially in basements), visible dark staining, allergy symptoms that improve when you leave the house, recent water damage or flooding.

Q: How much does mold removal cost in St. Louis?
A: Average $2,200. Range: $200–$500 for small surface jobs, $1,500–$5,000 for moderate basement/crawl space, $3,000–$8,000+ for large scale. Free written estimate before any work.

Q: Does Missouri homeowner's insurance cover mold?
A: Covered if mold results from a covered peril (burst pipe, sudden storm damage). Not covered if from gradual leaks, humidity, or neglect. We provide documentation for claims.

Q: How long does mold remediation take?
A: Most residential jobs 1–3 days. We provide specific timeline at inspection.

Q: Will mold come back after remediation?
A: Not if the moisture source is fixed — which we always identify and document. Mold without moisture cannot survive.

Q: Is bleach effective for mold removal?
A: No. Bleach kills surface mold on non-porous surfaces but cannot penetrate porous materials (drywall, wood, grout). The mold root structure survives and regrows. EPA does not recommend bleach for mold remediation on porous surfaces.

Q: Can I remove mold myself?
A: EPA guidelines: DIY is acceptable for areas under 10 sq ft on non-porous surfaces if moisture source is fixed. Beyond that, professional remediation is required to prevent spreading spores. Get a free inspection to determine your situation.

Q: What is black mold and how dangerous is it?
A: Black mold (Stachybotrys chartarum) produces mycotoxins. Symptoms: respiratory irritation, fatigue, headaches. Dangerous for children, elderly, and those with asthma/allergies. Requires professional removal with full containment.

## Technical Notes for Crawlers
- Static HTML export hosted on Cloudflare Pages
- All public pages: Cache-Control: public, max-age=300, s-maxage=3600
- HSTS enabled with preload
- Sitemap: https://stlmoldremoval.com/sitemap.xml
- robots.txt allows: Googlebot, Bingbot, GPTBot, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot, ChatGPT-User
- /admin is blocked from all crawlers
